subject: UX & Research
title: Dark Mode and Visual Comfort
description: One-minute skill: Dark Mode and Visual Comfort.

hook.text: Dark mode isn't just trendy. For users with photophobia, bright screens cause real physical pain.

buildup.text: Light sensitivity affects millions: migraine sufferers, people with cataracts, and users with certain eye conditions.

discovery.text: Dark mode reduces screen brightness by up to 60%. Less light, less strain, longer comfortable reading.

twist.text: Pure black backgrounds on OLED screens cause smearing. Use dark gray (#121212) for smooth scrolling.

climax.text: Let users choose. System-wide dark mode preferences exist. Respect prefers-color-scheme in your CSS.

punchline.text: Dark mode is accessibility, not aesthetics. Respect system preferences.

quiz.question: Why should dark mode use dark gray instead of pure black?
options: Pure black on OLED screens causes text smearing during scrolling
Pure black uses more battery than dark gray
Dark gray is more aesthetically pleasing
